channel_title: AI News & Strategy Daily | Nate B Jones
title: 8 Ways to Use AI When Someone Is Trying to Screw You (Adversarial Prompting)
executive_summary: AI is transforming how individuals can challenge established institutions by exposing hidden violations and decoding complex regulations. This shift allows ordinary people to gain leverage against systems that have historically favored the powerful. The video highlights how a family saved $160,000 by effectively using AI tools to investigate and challenge unfair billing practices.
bullets: AI is dismantling centuries-old systems of information asymmetry, allowing individuals to challenge institutions. This means that ordinary people can now uncover truths that were once hidden, similar to how a flashlight reveals hidden corners in a dark room.

Large language models can decode regulatory rulebooks instantly, making complex legal language accessible. This is like having a translator who can turn a foreign language into plain English in seconds, allowing you to understand the rules without years of study.

Adversarial prompting is a technique that helps individuals gain an advantage in real-world disputes. It’s like using a chess strategy to outsmart your opponent by anticipating their moves, giving you a tactical edge in negotiations.

AI exposes hidden violations in complex billing systems, which can lead to significant financial savings. Just like a detective finding clues in a mystery novel, AI can uncover discrepancies that lead to overcharges, saving families thousands of dollars.

The opportunity to learn how to use AI tools is critical before institutions fully adopt them. If you wait, it’s like trying to catch a train that has already left the station; you’ll miss out on the advantages these tools can provide.

A family used AI to save $160,000 by challenging unfair billing practices. This case illustrates how powerful AI can be when used correctly, akin to a superhero discovering their powers just in time to save the day.

AI does not provide legal advice but equips users with tools to uncover the truth. Think of it like giving someone a magnifying glass to find hidden details rather than telling them what those details mean.

The shift of leverage from institutions back to individuals is a game-changer in disputes. It’s like a seesaw where the weight has suddenly shifted, allowing the lighter side to rise and gain the upper hand.

AI can analyze vast amounts of data quickly, revealing patterns that humans might miss. This is similar to how a skilled chef can taste a dish and identify every ingredient, while a novice might only notice the overall flavor.

The video emphasizes the importance of using AI tools before institutions fully exploit them. If you don’t act quickly, it’s like being in a race where your competitors have a head start and you’re left trying to catch up.

AI can help individuals navigate complex systems that were previously inaccessible. It’s like having a GPS in a city you’ve never visited, guiding you through unfamiliar streets to reach your destination efficiently.

The video suggests that AI is leveling the playing field, making it easier for individuals to hold institutions accountable. This is like giving everyone a fair chance in a game where the rules were previously stacked against them.
key_takeaways: Learn how to use AI tools for investigation before institutions do. This is crucial because it allows you to uncover hidden truths and protect your interests. If you delay, you risk being outmaneuvered by those who already have a head start.

Set a rule that at least 50% of your evaluation tests must check for hidden violations in billing systems. This matters because many teams focus too heavily on functionality rather than potential discrepancies. If you don’t adjust this balance, you could miss significant savings.

Practice adversarial prompting techniques to gain leverage in disputes. This is important as it prepares you to outsmart opponents in negotiations. Without this practice, you may find yourself at a disadvantage when it matters most.

technical_terms.Adversarial Prompting: A technique used to gain an advantage in negotiations or disputes by anticipating the opponent's moves. Think of it like a chess player who plans several moves ahead to outsmart their opponent.
technical_terms.Large Language Models: AI systems that can understand and generate human-like text, making complex information easier to digest. Like a skilled translator who can turn complicated legal jargon into everyday language.
technical_terms.Information Asymmetry: A situation where one party has more or better information than another, often leading to unfair advantages. Think of it like a game of poker where one player can see everyone else's cards while others can only see their own.
technical_terms.Billing Systems: Complex frameworks used by companies to charge customers for services or products. Like a complicated recipe that requires precise measurements to ensure the dish turns out correctly.
technical_terms.Regulatory Rulebooks: Documents that outline the laws and regulations governing specific industries. Think of it like a rulebook for a board game that everyone must follow to play fairly.
